notes: Countersign level->scope policy. An earned autonomy license (L0..L4, see apps/janus/src/license.ts) is the ceiling on which capabilities (apps/janus/src/janus/capabilities.ts) a credential may unlock. Levels are CUMULATIVE: each list contains every capability unlockable at that level, including all lower levels, so L0 subset of L1 subset of L2 subset of L3 subset of L4. Membership is DERIVED from the catalog by (risk, sideEffecting): L0 Observe unlocks nothing. L1 Ask = low-risk, non-side-effecting discovery/read caps only (no prepare, no commit). L2 Recommend = L1 plus every remaining non-side-effecting cap (the *.prepare / *.draft / *.proposed staging caps plus medium-risk reads) — may PREPARE for human approval but still executes zero side-effects. L3 Guarded Act = L2 plus credential.scoped_request plus low-risk side-effecting commits only; medium/high/critical commits must escalate. L4 Limited Autonomy = L3 plus medium-risk side-effecting commits. INVARIANT (capability is not permission): high/critical side-effecting commits are listed in always_human_approval and are NEVER auto-unlocked by ANY level — a human must approve each one explicitly, even at L4. always_forbidden mirrors GLOBAL_FORBIDDEN: those caps are denied even with human approval. The manifest is hashed (see scopePolicy.manifestDigest) and the digest is embedded into Warrants so the level->scope policy is itself tamper-evident.
